Title: Co-administration of 20(S)-protopanaxatriol (g-PPT) and EGFR-TKI overcomes EGFR-TKI resistance by decreasing SCD1 induced lipid accumulation in non-small cell lung cancer

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: NSCLC patient tissues; HCC827; PC9; H1975; HCC827GR
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports lipid droplet accumulation and cancer cell survival/proliferation; associated with EGFR-TKI resistance
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monounsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis; lipid droplet-associated lipid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Stearate/palmitate desaturation branch leading to MUFA production
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Fatty acid desaturase; integral membrane desaturase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a double bond into stearic and palmitic acids to generate oleic acid and palmitoleic acid, respectively
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Intracellular lipid droplets in NSCLC tumor cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in post-EGFR-TKI patient tumor tissues and in EGFR-TKI-resistant NSCLC cell lines than in pre-treatment or sensitive counterparts; reduced after short-term gefitinib exposure; decreased by g-PPT
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in resistant H1975 and HCC827GR cells and post-TKI tumors; downregulated by g-PPT; transiently reduced after short-term gefitinib
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: EGFR-TKI treatment duration; oleic acid supplementation; g-PPT treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: IHC in paired patient tissues; Western blotting in cell lines; public transcriptome analysis (GSE83666, GSE38310); lipid droplet staining (Oil Red O, Nile red, BODIPY); xenograft IHC/IF under drug treatments
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High SCD1 expression promotes oleic acid-associated lipid droplet accumulation and EGFR-TKI resistance; inhibition of SCD1 by g-PPT lowers lipid droplets and restores gefitinib sensitivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SCD1 expression is elevated in post-TKI NSCLC tissues and in EGFR-TKI-resistant cell lines relative to sensitive counterparts. 2. Its enzymatic product oleic acid reduces the cytotoxicity of gefitinib and osimertinib and restores p-EGFR/AKT/ERK signaling. 3. g-PPT suppresses SCD1 expression, decreases lipid droplet accumulation, and synergizes with gefitinib to reverse resistance in vitro and in vivo.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point in abnormal lipid metabolism associated with EGFR-TKI resistance
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Inhibition of lipid metabolism may have context-dependent effects; in EGFR-TKI-sensitive PC9 cells, g-PPT plus gefitinib did not enhance apoptosis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper frames SCD1 as the central metabolism gene linking EGFR signaling, MUFA production, lipid droplet accumulation, and drug resistance; mechanistic discussion cites EGFR-mediated SCD1 stabilization from prior literature, but this paper mainly validates expression and phenotypic association.
